review_text: We spent two nights in a Delux cabin. Whilst the cabin is a little out dated, it was very comfy, tidy and had everything we needed (including an oven!). The park itself is lovely, walking distance to the SLC Cafe and beach, and a short drive into town which has IGA, butcher, bakery and other shops. The staff were very helpful with local attractions to see.
Lovely little cabin, (#10) very well laid out. Great orientation to enjoy late afternoon sun and a 2 minute walk to morning coffee and sunrise watching over East Beach.

Stayed two nights on river site 76 in our 8m long motorhome (including bikes on the back). Large sites but they are directly under large trees and the site is mostly dirt with some grass closer to water. Price was reasonable (Thurs/Fri night late March) and staff friendly and helpful but facilities are in really poor condition and certainly not that clean. Good location close to town and rail trail though.
Very nice ....
We didn't have much grass on our site but all In all a good park
Had a great time we stayed 2 nights love it thank you
This park is a delight. The cabins are well designed, immaculately clean and contains all the necessary equipment for a great holiday.

Park is in a great spot & Port Fairy is a lovely little town,one of the amenities blocks is due to be renovated in about 6 months and believe me it needs it but until then it would be nice if someone would maintain it,very dirty and water over the floor, showers dripping, toilets leaking doesn’t take much to fix it 🤷‍♀️
It's not a hotel it is a caravan park.

Lovely CVPark
Huge CVPark
Facilities are being updated in 2026 so will be even better.
Dump point, laundry, close to botanical gardens, close to the footy oval, powered and unpowered available.
Most sites are grassy.

Der Platz ist sehr groß. Nicht weit weg vom Strand und Stadt. Man kann gut dorthin gehen.
Die sanitären Anlagen sind schon recht alt, werden aber bald saniert. Das Wasser schmeckt fürchterlich. Es gibt nur eine Campkiitchen und sie ist weit weg, an der Rezeption. Für eine Übernachtung okay.

Currently staying at this Caravan Park - big sites by the Moyne River , easy walk to town or the beach . Great shops - so why only a 3
Terrible water pressure to the van !
No idea why - here with a group and others in our area have the same issue. Those on smaller sites in the middle seem to have normal flowing water . Showering etc very difficult

Location was great and we loved port fairy, however $60 for a patch of grass, no power, next to the bins with blinding lights is pretty steep.
The showers were also the dirtiest I've ever experienced in a caravan park. Felt pretty yucking trying to wash.
Great dog friendly park
Great for a camping trip or hire a cabin like we did

Excellent location.
Ample space and the disablity facilities worked great for me. I have my own travel comode but thought it was great the park had a few options for shower chairs
Great place to holiday.
Stayed 2 weeks plenty to do

I only stayed for one night at this campsite and while the grass itself for camping was good the facilities were very strange. In the bathrooms and showers and kitchen there was so many flies everywhere alive and dead like hundreds and hundreds and then there was heaps of mozzies too so if you like insects you should come but if you hate them I would stay away. Also the staff were very kind though
We love coming to Port Fairy, Vic and enjoy staying at this caravan park. It's close to town, easy to walk there rather than having to drive, and a great location to explore the surrounding region.
Loved it! Great facilities, lovely location a nice little walk into town and quick walk to the beach. Friendly staff 👍😊
Beautiful place. Amenities may need a little upgrade, but all in all, nice and clean. Lovely relaxing atmosphere and close to the beach and town
Pretty much self serve for caravan sites. Very casual but that's ok.

We had a site for our van over looking the river. Their sites are quite large, toilet blocks are kept clean just a lovely caravan park to stay. We stayed in the off peak season and the price was quite good. They also have a lot of carvings which were great to look at.
Lovely park near Lake. Amenities nice and clean.  Nice spot on the lake, plenty of room for the caravan and car. The sea isn't far away.
Good place and countryside
Well worth the stay. Lovely staff. Thank you

Great location! I got a cabin which was nice, ample space for 1 person, could have been a little cleaner but was enough for the night I stayed. Handy fish and chips across the road and a short walk to the beach and a bit longer walk into town or the river. I only stayed 1 night so didn't get to really get a feel for the place. Also located close the local footy oval. I didn't get to make use of the facilities but it looked like the bbq areas were nice and clean and I liked the layout of the park itself. Port fairy is a beutiful place and well worth a visit,  just wish the price was a little lower at this park.
We came here to ask for use of their dump station. Very friendly and helpful staff. Beautiful place, interesting wood sculptures. Highly recommended.

Doesn’t get any better than this. A beautiful park, can’t say it’s a little one because it’s bloody huge. And you get a football ground in the middle of a park. So got to watch the second half of the footy under lights after I set up. Beautiful ammenities that were very clean and a huge kitchen and rec room. This right next to the most gorgeous little town. Do yourself a favour and go here.
We stayed here for a few days in the caravan next to the water awesome place and a great town to explore
We loved our short stay here, such a beautiful place.  Quiet and relaxing.  We highly recommend 😊

Great sites, especially  site numbers 165-171.
Close to the beach 5 minute walk & 10 minute walk to shops.
Grat spot will definitely be back again next year..

We stayed in a dog friendly cabin which was great. Plenty of areas to walk the dog and a very well equipped, well organised park. Situated close to town. Would highly recommend. Will definitely be back when we feel like a quiet, relaxing break.
Dog friendly cabin moments from the beach - comfy, cozy and close to fabulous facilities
A really great location to camp!
Great place to visit , staff are super friendly, helpful, dog friendly , it’s a must to stay here , not far to the shops and places to see , rode out e bikes around and enjoyed every minute
Location is perfect, amenities are top notch, and camp sites have the best grass coverage. Great place all round.
Lots of grassy areas for camping. Camp kitchen close but shower block a bit of a walk.  Good rate for unpowered site. Quite busy.  Very close to beaches. Great place. I'd stay again.

It’s a really nice campground if you want to stay in the cute village Port Fairy. It’s really big and the next amenities can be quite far away. The toilets and showers are a bit old. It’s right on a sport field and the botanical garden. Just next time we would try to be closer to the amenities.
Pulled up with the caravan and setup for a short stay... Lovely location, close to town. It's a big park, great, helpful, friendly, accommodating staff. Good, clean amenities.
Caravan Site
Beautiful and Paceful Caravan. Pristine facilities

Great place, just across the road from the beach and next to the river. Close to the town of Port Fairy.
WiFi can be a bit hit and miss depending on your site because of the trees, and Satelite conne tin for TV the same.
Loved our stay here so much we extended, if you can get a spot under the pine trees overlooking the river. Sites are huge ., so much privacy Beautiful area , we rode our bikes everywhere.

Hugh grassy sites on the river and minutes from the beach. Lovely spot, easy walk into town.
Reception staff friendly and efficient. Our new favourite stop BTW Adelaide and Melbourne
Walking distance to a spectacular beach.

My family have been coming to the Gardens Caravan Park for nearly 30 years.
As we drove in this time for our stay, my wife said the park looks old & tired.
The park / gardens weren't up to previous years' standards. ( weeds & curbside gutters)
Lot of work going on,  but nothing seems to be accomplished.
Sites need leveling & topdressing. The fees go up, but there is no change to amenities or park overall appearance.
We love coming to Port Fairy & hope this review is taken as a well-intended comment, and you can get your act together.
Chris

Great location, amenities could be a bit cleaner but otherwise great stay.  Depends on where
you are in the park as to free wifi access.
Big Van Park, great location 👍

Booked online an unpowered campsite based on the pics provided. Expected a grassed area overlooking the river.

What we got was a small area between a basketball court, a building and someone’s house.  There was no way we would fit the car, the very small boat trailer and our tent in that spot.  Yes we booked the trailer too.  There were others camped in the same spot so we would have been squashed in with them.

Was offered a grassed river view spot for additional cost, however the “river” was low and no good for our fishing and boating, so we unfortunately couldn’t stay.

Large caravan park and very busy. Best to check before booking online.

Uncovered camp site overnight. Nice grassed areas. Clean camp kitchen and amenities. Cabins looked very nice. An oval and tennis court.  Nice hot showers. Reasonably priced. Note for owners... for older people, amenities a bit far from unpowered sites.

Facilities are old and dated, get like a sauna during the afternoon when it is hot.  Park itself is lovely.  We had a great stay, spacious site, some grass, some dirt.  Staff very friendly and helpful.  Port Fairy is a great spot.  If coming I would recommend the take away lamb from Conan’s Wine store, to die for and well worth the money!   But in general great park, busy whilst we were here with the sheepdog trials, which was great to check out.  We would recommend and would come again if back this. Way.
We were there in caravan in a great site on river.

Great Caravan/camping park destination.  Very spacious grounds with many other caravans and tent campers around but not too close to each other in early February. $96 for 2x nights powered. Enjoyed watching the Dog and Sheep competition over looking the large oval (part of the Reserve part of the park- site #129). I have previously stayed here many years ago in what is called the Gardens part of the park. I was in a tent that previous time, it was slightly closer to the town and we used the clean facilities/ camp kitchen/shower/ etc. This time we're in a RV and the experience is just as good. Walked into town easy 15mins. Enjoyed the shops in town etc. Ate at the very high quality Fish and chips shop near park entrance. Walked the very beautiful nearby beach. Although the roads to here are showing a lot of wear and tear it's only 3.5hours from Melbourne and Port Fairy is definitely a fine destination. Also The weather here is mild compared to the much hotter heat waves elsewhere in Victoria.

What a top spot to stay.A short walk to the beach. Close to shops and the park itself it really well looked after. It’s a great price as well. Dog friendly as well. They also have cabins which look great but we stayed on a pretty big powered site.|Over all fantastic.

Plenty of space to pitch a tent through school holidays which is great if you can be bothered dealing with 3,000 kids and families with loud music blaring, Unfortunately the park amenities are few and far between. No jumping pillow, no playgrounds which are decent and even finding a camp kitchen with a microwave, kettle or fridge doesn't exist apart from one which is to cater for over 5000 guests.
The free activity book for the kids is a tell tale sign, the campground should have enough entertainment for the kids rather than relying on a book.

Very close to the beach but sacrifices are to be had which doesn't make it remotely close to other sites.
It's cheap through school holidays but cheap for a reason. Overall 2/5.

Awesome caravan park. Facilities old but well maintained. Stayed in a caravan and got the BIGGEST and most AMAZING spot I have ever come across. It was huge, with river views and gorgeous pine trees for shade. To top of off the whole set was fully grassed. Absolute stunner of a spot.
No service. Food in the area was good.

What a perfect location. Just a short 3 min walk up to the most stunning beach.
Super clean facilities.
Our caravan site overlooking the lake and birdlife.
Loved it, & I'll be back!
What an amazing place to stay! Amenities are spotless and grounds are so well cared for. Thankyou for such friendly service as well.
Very friendly and good facilities

A Great Place to Stay in Port Fairy
I recently had the opportunity to stay at the Gardens Caravan Park in Port Fairy, and I was very impressed with my experience. The park is located in a great location, just a short walk from the beach and the town center. The facilities are clean and well-maintained, and the staff are very friendly and helpful.

I would highly recommend the Gardens Caravan Park to anyone looking for a clean, comfortable, and convenient place to stay in Port Fairy.

Here are some of the things I liked about the

Gardens Caravan Park:
* The location is excellent. The park is just a short walk from the beach, the town center, and the Botanic Gardens.

* The facilities are clean and well-maintained. The bathrooms and showers are spacious and clean, and the laundry facilities are convenient.

* The staff in the office were very helpful in the whole booking process. They also didn’t mind giving us some local information about what to see and do in Port Fairy.

* The park has a variety of activities for guests to enjoy, including swimming, fishing, boating, and hiking.

Overall, I had a great experience at the Gardens Caravan Park. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a clean, comfortable, and convenient place to stay in Port Fairy.
Here are some additional details that I think are important to mention:

* The park is open all year round.

* The park has a variety of accommodation options to choose from, including powered and unpowered sites, cabins, and caravans.

* The park has a range of amenities, including a camp kitchen, BBQs, laundry facilities, and playground.

* The park is located on the banks of the Moyne River, which provides guests with stunning views of the river and the surrounding countryside.

* The park is clean and well-maintained.

We will definitely be back here sometime in the near future.

Ali & Fiona
What a lovely park to enjoy some peaceful time it was a great week stay.  And it’s a lovely stroll to the beach and shopping

Huge caravan park,seems like we're the only ones here. Got no neighbours at all. Wendy at reception is amazing, wealth of knowledge about the area. Awesome fish n chop shop.just outside the gates. Old school fish n chips to die for omg. Short easy walk to town and beaches. Still here n loving it..

Friendly helpful staff - thanks for letting me know I left. Ventalin inhaler behind
Your care much appreciated
Cheers Michael
Wonderful staff. Great spots for vans along the river where we were. Amenities were pristine.
I’ve been here every year of my life and I love it. The spot we’re in amazing we’re so close to a park. Great beaches nearby the caravan park. I highly recommend coming here.

Cute cabin, warm and room for a family of 5 + dog friendly!  Great location, walk to town and surf beach.  Only issue was wifi did not work in my cabin which meant I had to sit on patio to do work on lap top - which was rather cold and inconvenient.  Also meant that the smart TV was not so smart as couldn't access any streaming or youtube.  Thankfully weather was not "Netflix and chill" weather!  Reported it to office but there was nothing they could do.  Room wasn't "cheap" so this was very disappointing.  Will check internet BEFORE checking in next time.
A bit expensive for an off season site

We stayed on a sites for Motorhome and Tent. Lovely location and park but security is lax as people walk through. Dump site is in need as hose too far from cleaning up. It is a lovely Town and park is huge
Great spot. Stayed on a powered site.

Stayed with friends a few months ago. Lovely location , on arrival the lady at reception, blonde Wendy gave us lots of information and places of interest in the area , really helpful.
The place was clean and comfortable.
I was in a caravan grassed site facing the river. Great caravan park
Great place to camp at Port Fairy. Large, grassy sites and easy access to the beach and town. Dog friendly.
Clean amenities, large powered sites
Toilets and shower block too far,not enough lighting in toilet and around oval.
Been going to gardens for 9 years brilliant park would recommend to anyone
Such a spacious caravan park that was great value with plenty of clean amenities. Loved it ♥️

Great location - large spaces. Would have been a nice stay if it weren’t for a travelling golf group who decided to keep half the park awake with unnecessary ‘carry on’ and noise past the quite time cut off. The communal sheds are not a place to party..

Couldn’t find a complaint portal via the website so just had to put up with it.

A lovely spot to spend a few days, however the facilities could do with a proper deep pressure clean and the toilet paper in the amenities block ran out twice during our stay during the day. This needs to be more regularly checked.

Although the park doesn’t have extras such as a pool, the location makes up for it. It’s just a short walk to beautiful East Beach and about a kilometre from the centre of Port Fairy. We stayed in a powered site which comfortably accommodated our large caravan, awning and 4WD. The amenities could do with being modernised but they are very clean. Within the park there are open green spaces for children to play or a large family group to gather. For holders of a senior’s card, a discount is offered. We will definitely stay here again if travelling this way.
Very clean and tidy, clean facilities.

Booked at short notice, very pleasant staff happy to help and give advice. Great location, pet friendly. Lovely Park and facilities, easy access to beach and local chip shop not far to town easy 15 to 20 min walk over bridge depending on pace.

Beautiful caravan park, friendly people, lush greenery and views, wake each morning to the sound of lovely birds and fresh ocean air. Amenities perfectly suitable. Price very fair for the weekend we stayed.
Wir waren mit unserem Motorhome für eine Nacht vor Ort. Wir hatten eine powered Site. Sie war grosszügig vom Platz her und schön wie auch ruhig gelegen. Die WCs und Duschen waren sauber.
Fantastic location close to beach and town , staff are very helpful and listened to my suggestions from last visit and installed more coat hooks and chairs in disabled bathroom. Very clean toilet.
Lovely spot - close to town - walking distance to great coffee . A clean beach across the street . Some sites have river views

Perfect location and super helpful staff. A short walk into town, an even shorter one to Eaat Beach. The staff were obliging and went the extra mile when we had some issues with the weather during a ferocious storm.

The grounds are very nice with a mixture of different sites.

The facilities could do with a bit of an update, though clean,  they are looking a little tired compared to other places we have stayed.

Large council park wraps around a sports oval. Lots of amenities blocks but ho-hum on the cleaning front (very on the nose). Great location with large grassy sites, some under Norfolk Pine trees if you prefer shade. Spectacular beach just a short walk away.

Top Stellplatz, waren begeistert. Alles sehr sauber , Wahnsinns Landschaft , schönes Städchen , jederzeit gerne wieder . Hier haben wir von nem netten Australier den Tipp bekommen zu den Grampians zu fahren
Went there to watch the sheepdog comp. Great afternoon.
Fantastic safe holiday park
Very dusty pitch, only one sink for washing up,on this massive site.

Have just spent one week in a caravan at this caravan park
Fabulous location
Friendly responsive staff in the office
Amenities were very very clean
Could not fault .We loved our getaway and will return

Only selected 1 start so I could write a review to warn people of the horrific  health hazard this park is ! It has the dirtiest amenities along side Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. Read below before you are exposed  !

The amenities are the dirtiest amenities I have ever seen the showers are filthy , full of grime soap scum fungus black mould and pubic hair . In a 2 week stay we left with in 2 days as we could not clean ourselves or use the facilities as the amenities where lower then the pits of a 3rd world country . In the 2 days the smell of body odour increased in the toilets where filthy with bodily fluids on the seats floors and walls it also has a damp mould dirty smell and this would be from the mould harbouring in the showers  . I did not witness , ANY cleaning nor did anyone else The amenities themselves are worse than ones you would see in an old dirty prison film including the festering filth of the drains the drains where brown black full and overflowing they also had a foul smell . The women’s amenities has a man hole completely open with no protection from the attic of the roof above one of the showers ! It is an absolute disgrace the handles and taps where full of soap scum , dirt , toilet paper clumps and dirty water . I personally contacted the health department as I am horrified & currently treating myself from a fungal infection and Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. If you don’t know what that is, it is a staph infection that is crated from bacteria that is harboured in a damp communal areas such as a public showers or toilets ! It is filthy disgusting and horrible ! Don’t waste your time
Warm welcome very helpful office staff. Muddy Unpowerd site no much grass filthy and smelly amenities.
Top joint.
Great friendly park and welcoming for others in related parks to use the facilities occasionally. Thank you.
I don't understand this yearly pilgrimage to a tent city, but it's okay.
Cabin was dirty, needed cleaning

We stayed for 4 nights, but left 1 night early as bad weather was coming in. Great park, well located, good site fees, but unfortunately this was our 2nd visit to Port Fairy and both times it has blown a Gail for our entire stay, disappointing as it's a lovely place to visit otherwise.

We've stayed here twice, once in our tent and the 2nd time we booked a pet friendly cabin.  The cabin was in a great spot and had everything we needed.  It was clean and well maintained.  The holiday park is great.  It's huge with lots of options for caravans and tents and lovely facilities.  It's a quick walk across the road and up a little hill to the beach and a lovely cafe where we got our morning coffee every day.
The staff at the holiday park are lovely, very friendly and helpful (thanks Wendy and Danielle).  We've already booked our next stay and can't wait to come back.
Beautiful town is a quick 10 minute walk away. Great location and caravan park has lots of space and clean amenities.
Lovely van park close to town. Nice sites with pleasant surrounds. Friendly staff. Nice amenities block. Had a nice 3 day stay here. Port Fairy is a lovely place to visit.
Very relaxed atmosphere, good facilities
Great caravan Park, magnificent location, friendly staff, and most importantly dog friendly! we will be back!!
Clean and tidy

We stayed here for 3 nights in our caravan.
The facilities and location are fantastic with amazing sunset.
Highly recommend this caravan park.

Easy walk to shops and cafes.
Big sites and lots of them.
Wonderful staff: when a colleague on a work trip left an electric toothbrush then went back to Melbourne, they actually delivered the lost item to me in Warrnambool!
Huge caravan park in central position in historic Port Fairy.

Tidy site, a bit on the costly side, well maintained facilities. Check in and out is completely remote, drive in and out without needing to get to the office once. Good location close to the beach and town.
Very pretty area with nice tree trunk wood carvings. Whale, seals and dolphins etc. Reception staff was very friendly and helpful
Great big sites, good location by the water. Friendly staff. Lots of space for the kids to run around. Close park to town. Very close to the beach.

Very nice place
And the staff were very helpful and friendly

Awesome place to kick your boots off read a book go for a walk and check out what Port fairy has to off a must when visiting this beautiful area of regional Victoria 😉!!
PS the staff are super friendly and approachable for anything you need  just ask Jason and the team!!
We are staying in our caravan so having checked any accommodation rooms
Fantastic camp-site.  Clean, spacious and beautiful setting.  Nothing else needs to be said!!
Very accommodating, clean and convenient.  The staff were very attentive to my requests. Can see why it gets so busy over the Christmas break down here.
Beautiful place  to stay. Nice and quiet

Great local pet friendly caravan park which is right beside the river in Port Fairy.

Friendly staff and great facilities make this a very enjoyable place to stay when in the area.
